axis_a.name: static source surface — definitions + registration + dispatch (all in tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa)

definitions.extra_law: half_life value MUST end in d|h|m|s time unit

axis_b.name: runtime live verification — selftest + emission shape

data.selftest_banner: OK — 42 strategies (16 baseline + raw 109-117 P1-P8 + 5-check + raw 118-134 Q1-Q8 + T1.3 + R1-R8), Tier 1/2/3 + raw 117/126 cross-cuts verified

axis_c.name: spec-correspondence — lint field+marker keys vs .raw scope wording

comparisons.interpretation: lint uses abbreviated field key (`self-app-witness` not `self-application-witness`) AND replaces narrative markers (step_1_witness/...) with operationalized ones (input_hash/output_hash/fixpoint_status). A spec-conforming author would write `self-application-witness:` and the lint would silently see no field — Tier-3 check returns [] (empty hits), passing vacuously. Honest observation: lint and spec disagree on the canonical key.

comparisons.byte_eq_markers: abbreviation_only — spec uses kebab-case half-life inside a sentence; lint requires snake_case half_life= as marker — orthographic conversion only
comparisons.interpretation: field key matches; marker tokens are snake-case operational forms. Acceptable abbreviation discipline.

field key matches; marker set DIVERGES — lint replaces (actual_intervention, counterfactual_no_intervention_estimate, counterfactual_method, p_value_or_effect_size) with (intervention, no_intervention_outcome, divergence_metric). Author writing the spec-canonical 5-marker form would partially match (actual_outcome only) and silently lose 4/5 marker checks.

field key matches; markers are abbreviated AND value allowlists DIVERGE — lint allowlist {full, bounded, level_k, qre} for `rationality`; spec values {fully_rational, bounded_rational, learning_dynamics} for `rationality_assumption`. Spec author writing `rationality_assumption=fully_rational` would (a) miss the lint's `rationality=` marker check and (b) fail the lint's `full|bounded|level_k|qre` allowlist.

comparisons.byte_eq_markers: partial (2/3 lint markers present in spec)
comparisons.interpretation: field key matches; lint adds `flow_direction` (not in spec, but a reasonable RG-physics axis) and drops `scale_transformation`/`irrelevant_operators`/`universality_class`/`critical_exponents`. Mostly subset+rename.

data.verdict: fail (F7 FAIL — lint marker keys diverge from .raw scope wording for 4/5 raws; raw 119 alone aligns; obstruction O1)

'hexa-only additive' is trivially satisfied: nothing was emitted that violates additive-first.
data.verdict: ok (IMPL_PRESENT — additive trivially holds because no edit needed)

options_considered.label: definition + registration in _strategies()
options_considered.rejected_rationale: still insufficient — _strategies() returns names but does not route them; would falsely PASS

options_considered.label: definition + registration + dispatch in _dispatch_tier3()
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — this is the minimum complete surface. F1+F2+F3 verify all three axes; only when all three PASS is the fn actually invoked at runtime.

Together, F1+F2+F3+F4 form a redundant 4-way confirmation that each of the 5 _check fns is real, enumerated, routed, AND exercised at runtime.

options_considered.label: patch the lint in this cycle to match spec wording

options_considered.label: demote the lint's existing markers to optional/warn-on-presence-only

axes_surfaced.decision: B — log F7 as obstruction O1 with full cross-reference, defer to refactor cycle
axes_surfaced.rationale: F7 FAIL is the most important honest observation in this cycle. The existing _check fns RUN and would emit warnings if their (lint-side) markers are missing — but the .raw spec text declares DIFFERENT markers. A future author writing a spec-conforming declaration (e.g., `self-application-witness: { step_1_witness=..., step_2_witness=... }`) would have ZERO of those markers caught by the lint, because the lint keys against `self-app-witness` with markers {step_N, input_hash, output_hash, fixpoint_status}. The lint is internally coherent and its selftest passes, but it audits an internal vocabulary that diverges from the spec. This is IMPORTANT and deserves explicit obstruction status, not silent acceptance.

obstructions.title: spec-correspondence drift — lint marker keys diverge from .raw scope wording for 4/5 targeted raws
obstructions.evidence: axis_c.comparisons (5 cross-reads). raw 118: lint key `self-app-witness` vs spec `self-application-witness` (byte-unequal); markers fully renamed (step_N/input_hash/output_hash/fixpoint_status vs step_1_witness/step_2_witness/step_n_pass_or_diverge/fixpoint_step_or_unbounded). raw 120: field key matches but markers reduce 5→4 with renames (intervention vs actual_intervention; no_intervention_outcome vs counterfactual_no_intervention_estimate; divergence_metric net-new; counterfactual_method + p_value_or_effect_size dropped). raw 127: rationality value allowlist {full, bounded, level_k, qre} disagrees with spec {fully_rational, bounded_rational, learning_dynamics} — even sharing zero overlap. raw 128: lint adds `flow_direction` not in spec; drops scale_transformation/irrelevant_operators/universality_class/critical_exponents. Only raw 119 aligns.
obstructions.impact: A future author writing a spec-canonical declaration (e.g., the exact text from .raw scope clause) would NOT be flagged correctly — the lint would either return [] (vacuous PASS) for the divergent field key (raw 118) or partial-marker hits for the divergent marker sets (raw 120/127/128). The lint and the spec are both internally coherent but mutually disagreeing on canonical vocabulary.
obstructions.suggested_close: separate refactor cycle — either (a) rename lint keys+markers to byte-match .raw scope wording (additive-disciplined: add new keys as aliases, deprecate old after streak), OR (b) amend .raw scope clauses to byte-match lint vocabulary (justified if lint vocabulary is more operationalized — input_hash/output_hash/fixpoint_status are testable; step_1_witness/step_2_witness are narrative). Decision belongs to a follow-up cycle with explicit cross-team review.

fixpoint_marker: impl-present-attestation-converged for the kick topic's 5 named raws (118/119/120/127/128). The 5 _check fns ARE defined, registered, dispatched, runtime-attested via selftest, and shape-contract-conformant (T1.1-T1.4 PASS, F1-F6 PASS). The single F7 FAIL (spec-correspondence drift on 4/5 raws) is logged as obstruction O1 with full cross-reference evidence — it does NOT block this cycle's PASS verdict because the topic scope is impl-presence not spec-byte-eq, and the lint is internally coherent + selftest-attested. NO source code change to tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa needed; NO new raw rule needed; the 5 _check subroutines are stable on their own internal vocabulary, with a known divergence ticket (O1) for the next cycle.
